audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Thessalonians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for Timothy mission, afflictions/pressure, concern over testing, encouragement, prayer for reunion, love increase, holiness.

verses.restored: Therefore, no longer enduring it, we thought it good to be left behind in Athens alone.
And we sent Timothy, our sibling and coworker of the Divine in the good news of Messiah, to establish and encourage you concerning your trust,
so that no one be shaken in these pressures; for you yourselves know that we are appointed for this.
For even when we were with you, we told you beforehand that we were about to be pressured, just as also it happened and you know.
Because of this, I also, no longer enduring it, sent to know your trust, lest somehow the testing one had tested you and our labor had become empty.

But now Timothy, having come to us from you and having announced good news to us of your trust and love, and that you have good remembrance of us always, longing to see us just as we also long to see you,
because of this, siblings, we were comforted/encouraged over you in all our necessity and pressure through your trust.
Because now we live, if you stand firm in the Lord.
For what thanksgiving are we able to repay to the Divine concerning you, for all the joy with which we rejoice because of you before our Divine,
night and day praying beyond measure to see your face and to complete what is lacking in your trust?
Now may our Divine and Father himself, and our Lord Jesus, direct our way to you.
And may the Lord make you increase and abound in love toward one another and toward all, just as also we toward you,
so as to establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our Divine and Father at the presence/arrival of our Lord Jesus with all his holy ones.

translation_notes: Thlipsis is rendered pressure to preserve the social and spiritual intensity of affliction without romanticizing suffering.
The testing language is kept without overdeveloping later demonology; the focus remains on the community's trust standing under pressure.
The closing prayer ties love-abounding to holiness, not moralism detached from relationship.
